title: Add path allowlist validation for bash tool (parity with CC checkPathConstraints)
description:
## Gap

CC's checkPathConstraints (tools/BashTool/pathValidation.ts:1013-1100) validates that file paths accessed by path-modifying commands (cd, mv, cp, rm, ln, tee, truncate, install, find, sed, rsync) are within the allowed working directories for the session. READONLY_COMMAND_REGEXES (readOnlyValidation.ts:1509-1570) classifies cd, ls, find and other commands as read-only when they match safe patterns, and validates cd paths against the allowed directory list.

OC has no path allowlist. execute_bash and BackgroundShellManager::spawn pass commands directly to bash -c. Any path accessible to the OC process can be written or deleted if the command passes the denylist.

Key behaviors missing:
- Allowed working directory set (per-session, multiple dirs supported)
- cd path resolution and validation against allowed set
- Compound command write + cd block (prevents cd /secret && mv ...)
- Redirect target path validation (> /etc/passwd blocked)
- Process substitution >(...) blocked
- Windows UNC path detection

## Parity reference
- CC: tools/BashTool/pathValidation.ts:1013 checkPathConstraints, readOnlyValidation.ts:1509 READONLY_COMMAND_REGEXES
- OC: missing entirely — src/tools/bash/policy.rs has no path awareness

## Acceptance
- Per-session allowed working directory set (initially = cwd at session start)
- Validate redirect targets and path arguments for path-modifying commands
- Block compound cd + write patterns
- Return error (not panic) when path is outside allowed set
